title: Lucky Loser Vekic Beats Raducanu to Rule Queen’s Club
description: World No. 76 Donna Vekic won her fifth title and first in three years.

title: Majchrzak beats three top-10 players in a row to win a maiden title in ‘s-Hertogenbosch
description: Three top-10 wins in a row, a maiden title at 30 and tears on court: Kamil Majchrzak beat Alex de Minaur 6-3, 2-6, 7-6 (5) to win in 's-Hertogenbosch and climb to a career high inside the top 50.

title: Six Pack: Shelton Dethrones Fritz for Sixth Career Title in Stuttgart
description: Ben Shelton is the first American man in 16 years to win titles on three different surfaces.

title: Donna Vekic denied Emma Raducanu when it mattered most in London
description: Emma Raducanu was trying to claim her second title of her career by beating Donna Vekic in the final of the HSBC Championships. Things didn't start as planned.

title: Lucky loser Vekic completes an improbable week with the Queen’s title
description: She failed to qualify, got in only as a lucky loser, and walked away with the trophy: Donna Vekic beat Emma Raducanu 6-0, 7-6 (6) to win at Queen's for her first title since 2023 and a climb back to No 32.

title: What Ben Shelton just accomplished could change everything at Wimbledon
description: Two Americans with high hopes for Wimbledon faced off in the final of the Boss Open. Here is what happened between Taylor Fritz and Ben Shelton.

title: Shelton joins Alcaraz, Djokovic and Bublik with clay, grass and hard titles in one season as he takes Stuttgart
description: Three sets every match, the first set dropped every time, and the title at the end of it: Ben Shelton beat Taylor Fritz 6-4, 2-6, 6-4 to win in Stuttgart for a first grass-court crown and a third title of 2026, extending his winning run over Fritz to three.

title: BBVA Open Internacional De Valencia: Tomova makes last four
description: Viktoriya Tomova won against Darja Semenistaja 6-3, 6-1 on Friday and will face the winner of the match between Brazilian Laura Pigossi and Belarusian Iryna Shymanovich in the next round

description: In two warm-up events for Wimbledon, the HSBC Championships and Boss Open finals have shaped up to be brilliant. Here is who should win.

title: Resurgent Raducanu Wins Twice to Race Into Queen’s Club Final
description: Emma Raducanu scored two wins in one day to reach her third career final in London.

title: Emma Raducanu on the cusp of doing something she hasn't done since the US Open
description: Emma Raducanu hasn't won a title since the US Open in 2021, but after taking the hard way through the HSBC Championships, she's close to a second.

title: Majchrzak Stuns Medvedev to Reach ‘s-Hertogenbosch Final
description: Kamil Majchrzak stunned Daniil Medvedev to reach his first career final on Saturday in 's-Hertogenbosch.

title: Thriller! Shelton Saves Match Points, Sets Up All-American Stuttgart Final vs. Fritz
description: Top seeded Ben Shelton saved two match points and will play for his third title of 2026.

title: Majchrzak topples Medvedev to reach a maiden ATP final
description: A second top-10 scalp of the week carried him to a career first: world No 76 Kamil Majchrzak beat Daniil Medvedev 7-6 (4), 6-1 to reach a maiden ATP final, where Alex de Minaur awaits.

title: 484-Ranked Montgomery Reaches First WTA Final
description: 21-year-old Robin Montgomery reached her first career final on the grass in the Netherlands.

title: “There’s a lot of ways to win a match, I’ve been choosing the most difficult” – Shelton survives match points again to reach the Stuttgart final
description: Three sets every match, a first set dropped every time, and two match points saved in a 16-14 tie-break: Ben Shelton (No 1) beat Jiri Lehecka 6-7 (4), 7-6 (14), 7-6 (6) to reach his first grass-court final, where Taylor Fritz awaits.

title: De Minaur reels off 10 straight games to reach the ‘s-Hertogenbosch final
description: Level at 2-4, then untouchable: Alex de Minaur (No 2) won the last 10 games to beat Adrian Mannarino 6-4, 6-0 and reach his 21st career final, eyeing a Dutch double after Rotterdam.

title: Shiny, happy Raducanu storms into her biggest final since the 2021 US Open
description: Not a set dropped all week and her biggest final since New York in 2021: Emma Raducanu (No 42) beat Iva Jovic 6-2, 6-2 to reach the Queen's final, where Donna Vekic awaits.

title: Montgomery and Krejcikova to meet for the ‘s-Hertogenbosch title
description: World No 484 Robin Montgomery beat Ajla Tomljanovic 6-4, 6-2 to reach the Libéma Open final, where eighth seed Barbora Krejcikova — a 6-3, 7-6 (4) winner over Magda Linette — awaits.

title: Raducanu battles past resilient Rakhimova to reach the Queen’s last four
description: A flypast, a slip and a thigh strapping could not stop her: Emma Raducanu (No 42) beat Kamilla Rakhimova 6-3, 7-5 to battle into the Queen's semi-finals, where Iva Jovic awaits.
